name: South Karelia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Karelian arms
the golden crown and sword hilts of the Karelian arms
the black wavy base beneath the arms

description: A square red flag charged with the arms of Karelia — two silver armoured arms grasping a sword and a scimitar raised toward a golden crown — over a black wavy base.
history: South Karelia (Etelä-Karjala) is one of the regions established in Finland's 1997 reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, which itself draws on the historic provincial arms of Karelia documented from the late sixteenth century.

features.role: a black wavy base across the lower part of the flag

features.symbolism: two crowned arms emerging from hoist and fly — the upper a gauntleted arm with a straight Western sword, the lower a mail arm with a Russian scimitar — in silver beneath a golden crown, symbolising the region's long position between the Western and Eastern spheres.
